summary: American postmodernist author and essayist. Most commonly identified by the novel Infinite Jest (Enfield Tennis Academy, Hal Incandenza, addictive 'Entertainment' film, Quebecois Wheelchair Assassins) and essays 'A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again' (Caribbean cruise on the Nadir), 'Consider the Lobster,' and 'This Is Water' (Kenyon commencement speech). Also wrote The Pale King (unfinished, IRS in Peoria), The Broom of the System, and 'E Unibus Pluram.'

works.name: Infinite Jest
works.description: Wallace's massive 1996 novel set at the Enfield Tennis Academy (ETA) near Boston. Hal Incandenza (memorizes the OED, smokes weed in tunnels) is the son of James Incandenza, who creates 'the Entertainment,' an addictive film whose viewers lose all interest in anything else. Quebecois Wheelchair Assassins (A.F.R.) seek the film; U.S.O.U.S. officer Hugh Steeply (often disguised as 'Helen') liaises with double-agent Remy Marathe. Don Gately at Ennet House Drug and Alcohol Recovery House. Joelle van Dyne (Prettiest Girl of All Time, 'Madame Psychosis') runs a radio show '60 min +/-' while wearing a veil. Michael Pemulis is the greatest Eschaton player. Other characters: John 'No Relation' Wayne, Ortho 'The Darkness' Stice. 388 endnotes under 'Notes and Errata' including Incandenza's complete filmography. Wikipedia

titled for a video called 'The Entertainment' whose viewers become so engrossed that they forget to do anything but watch it

works.name: Essays
works.description: Wallace's essays are heavily clued. 'A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again' (cruise on Zenith renamed Nadir; Nigel Ellery hypnotist; Tibor waiter). 'Consider the Lobster' (first published in Gourmet; Maine Lobster Festival ethics). 'This Is Water' (Kenyon commencement 2005; two-fish parable). 'E Unibus Pluram' (TV's influence on fiction; Don DeLillo's barn from White Noise). 'Authority and American Usage' (lexicographer Bryan Garner). 'Big Red Son' (Adult Video News Awards at Caesars Palace). 'Lynch Keeps His Head' (David Lynch / Blue Velvet). 'Up, Simba' (John McCain campaign). 'Derivative Sport in Tornado Alley' (Gil Antitoi / fence / tennis in Champaign). 'Good Old Neon': narrator says 'my whole life I've been a fraud.' Brief Interviews with Hideous Men (Q-and-A format). Wallace also wrote a senior thesis critiquing Richard Taylor's sea battle / fatalism argument using 'System J.'

clues.clue: 'A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again': Caribbean cruise; ship Zenith renamed Nadir; hypnotist Nigel Ellery; 'Professional Smile'
examples: his unpleasant trip on the cruise ship Zenith, which he renames the Nadir
In one of many footnotes, that essay's author dubs one of these places Nadir instead of Zenith

clues.clue: 'Consider the Lobster': ethics of boiling animals alive at the Maine Lobster Festival; first published in Gourmet magazine; notes lobsters have nociceptors but cannot make endorphins
examples: an essay contemplates the plight of animals that flail around helplessly when placed in boiling water
In this essay that Wallace first published in Gourmet magazine, the neurobiology of the title creature is detailed

clues.clue: 'This Is Water': Kenyon College commencement 2005; two fish and older fish parable; 'the only thing capital-T True is how you choose to think'; 'simple awareness'

In this speech's opening 'didactic little parable,' two young fish are confused by an older fish

David Foster Wallace's essay 'E Unibus Pluram' praises the 'complicated ironies' of a lengthy passage about this location

examples: David Foster Wallace's essay 'Big Red Son,' which documents the 1998 Adult Video News Award

works.name: The Pale King
works.description: Wallace's unfinished posthumous novel (2011), edited by Michael Pietsch, set largely at an IRS office in Peoria, Illinois. Characters include 'Irrelevant' Chris Fogle (turns pages, signs Memo 20s), David Cusk (fear of sweating causes constant sweating), and the ghost of a mirror inspector who hanged himself. D.T. Max's biography Every Love Story is a Ghost Story chronicles Wallace's life. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Unfinished posthumous novel (2011); IRS office in Peoria, Illinois; 'Irrelevant' Chris Fogle; edited by Michael Pietsch

examples: D. T. Max's biography of this author, Every Love Story is a Ghost Story

works.name: The Broom of the System
works.description: Wallace's debut novel about Lenore Beadsman, switchboard operator at publisher Frequent and Vigorous, who owns a talking bird named Vlad the Impaler / Ugolino the Significant and tries to find her missing grandmother who escaped a nursing home. Publisher Rick Vigorous receives stories. Lenore visits her brother 'Antichrist' in Massachusetts. Stonecipheco company builds a pineal-gland additive for baby food; another company builds a desert in Ohio. Title refers to a Wittgenstein riddle about whether a broom's handle or bristles are more useful. Wikipedia

examples: a publisher named Rick Vigorous receives a story about a man with 'second-order vanity'

examples: In this novel, a character orders nine steaks at a restaurant as part of his plan to become so fat that he takes up the entire universe

comprehensive_summary:
David Foster Wallace (1962–2008) is a defining figure of late-20th-century American postmodernist literature. Infinite Jest (1996) is his primary quizbowl identifier. Set at the Enfield Tennis Academy near Boston, it follows Hal Incandenza — a tennis prodigy who memorizes the OED and smokes weed in underground tunnels — whose father James created 'the Entertainment' (also called 'the samizdat'), a film so addictive that viewers lose all interest in anything else. James committed suicide by microwaving his head; the Entertainment was his attempt to cure Hal's anhedonia. The Quebecois Wheelchair Assassins (A.F.R.) seek the film; U.S.O.U.S. officer Hugh Steeply (often disguised as a woman named 'Helen') liaises with double-agent Remy Marathe. Don Gately stays at the Ennet House Drug and Alcohol Recovery House. Joelle van Dyne (the 'Prettiest Girl of All Time' / PGOAT) stars in the film and runs a radio show '60 min +/-' under the name 'Madame Psychosis' while veiling her face. Michael Pemulis is the greatest Eschaton player; Eschaton degenerates into chaos over the map-territory relation. Orin Incandenza (deformed Mario's brother, Phoenix Cardinals punter) traps cockroaches under upside-down glasses. The novel has 388 endnotes under 'Notes and Errata,' including James Incandenza's complete filmography. Chronological markers include the 'Year of the Depend Adult Undergarment' and the 'Year of Glad' (Subsidized Time). The closing scene shows Hal unable to communicate, possibly due to the drug DMZ. James Wood coined 'hysterical realism' for Infinite Jest and Zadie Smith's White Teeth.

Wallace's essays are equally important. 'A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again' describes a Caribbean cruise on a ship he renames the Nadir (from Zenith); features hypnotist Nigel Ellery and waiter Tibor. 'Consider the Lobster,' first published in Gourmet magazine, contemplates the ethics of boiling animals alive at the Maine Lobster Festival, noting that lobsters have nociceptors but cannot produce endorphins. 'This Is Water,' his 2005 Kenyon College commencement address, opens with the parable of two young fish and an older fish and argues that 'the only thing capital-T True is how you choose to think.' 'E Unibus Pluram' traces television's influence on American fiction (praising the 'complicated ironies' in DeLillo's 'most photographed barn' passage from White Noise). 'Authority and American Usage' calls lexicographer Bryan Garner — who synthesizes descriptivism and prescriptivism — a genius, using 'SNOOT' in all caps. 'Big Red Son' covers the Adult Video News Awards at Caesars Palace (described as 'as big as 20 Wal-Marts'). 'Lynch Keeps His Head' discusses David Lynch and Blue Velvet. 'Up, Simba' covers John McCain's 2000 presidential campaign for Rolling Stone (later published as a book titled for McCain's campaign bus). 'Derivative Sport in Tornado Alley' describes playing tennis in Champaign, Illinois and being blown into a fence with Gil Antitoi. A 1997 review dismissed John Updike as 'a penis with a thesaurus' and 'Champion Literary Phallocrat.' Brief Interviews with Hideous Men uses Q-and-A format and includes 'Forever Overhead' (second-person, 13th birthday at a swimming pool) and 'The Depressed Person.' 'Good Old Neon' opens with 'my whole life I've been a fraud' and ends with the narrator driving into a bridge abutment; it was written after the death of high school classmate Neal and is collected in Oblivion. His senior thesis critiqued Richard Taylor's fatalism argument about sea battles using a Montague grammar-derived 'System J.'

The Pale King (2011), his unfinished posthumous novel edited by Michael Pietsch, is set at an IRS office in Peoria, Illinois; characters include 'Irrelevant' Chris Fogle (turns pages, signs Memo 20s) and David Cusk (fear of sweating causes constant sweating). D.T. Max's biography Every Love Story is a Ghost Story chronicles Wallace's life.

The Broom of the System, his debut novel, follows Lenore Beadsman — switchboard operator at publisher Frequent and Vigorous — who owns a talking bird named Vlad the Impaler (also Ugolino the Significant) and seeks her missing grandmother who escaped a nursing home. Publisher Rick Vigorous receives stories about 'second-order vanity.' Lenore visits her brother nicknamed 'Antichrist' in Massachusetts. A character orders nine steaks to become so fat he takes up the entire universe. Stonecipheco builds a pineal-gland additive for baby food; another company builds a desert in Ohio. The title refers to a Wittgenstein riddle about whether a broom's handle or bristles are more useful. David Lipsky's Although Of Course You End Up Becoming Your Self documents the Infinite Jest tour. Wallace died by suicide in 2008.
